data.title: Schwab's Kevin Gordon says investors are rewarding S&P 500 earnings beats less
data.description: According to CNBC, S&P 500 companies that beat Wall Street earnings estimates are being rewarded less than usual this season, with the average stock in that group slipping about 0.2% the day after reporting, according to a Charles Schwab data analysis. In an average quarter since 2017, such companies have gained about 0.6% after earnings, and Kevin Gordon, head of macro research and strategy at the Schwab Center for Financial Research, said investors appear to be taking profits on some of the market's high flyers after results. He said the pullback looks more like a pause than a broader warning sign, noting that earnings breadth remains strong and that traders should view conditions as highly rotational rather than correctional. Gordon added that more than 87% of the nearly 16% of S&P 500 companies that had reported by Wednesday afternoon beat expectations, according to FactSet data. He said he will watch consumer-facing companies for signs of how shoppers are coping with higher energy prices and the prospect of higher borrowing costs, while next week is set to be the busiest of the season, with more than one-third of the S&P 500 scheduled to report, including Amazon, Apple and Ford.

data.title: OpenAI Infrastructure Chief: Company Started Using AMD Helios GPU Racks Three Months Ago
data.description: According to Jin10, OpenAI's infrastructure chief said the company started using AMD Helios GPU racks three months ago.

data.title: JPMorgan says AI-themed ETFs rank among top five themes by assets
data.description: According to CNBC, J.P. Morgan Asset Management said ETFs that offer investors exposure to artificial intelligence are among the top five themes by assets under management, even as the group saw volatility in the second quarter. Jon Maier, the firm's chief ETF strategist, said many themes are shifting toward AI and the broader ecosystem around it, including applications, energy and AI models. He also said mutual fund inflows have been tapering off while more money is moving into ETFs, and argued that ETFs are becoming more attractive to retail investors because of tax benefits.

data.title: US to Tighten Car Door and Handle Rules After Tesla Deaths
data.description:
US auto safety regulators will begin developing new requirements aimed at ensuring drivers and passengers can safely exit their vehicles after sometimes deadly incidents in which people became trapped inside cars, according to Bloomberg.

The move is intended to address safety issues highlighted by Tesla deaths.

data.title: Kuwait's Foreign Ministry Condemns Ongoing Attacks by Iran and Its Proxies
data.description: Kuwait's Foreign Ministry condemned the ongoing attacks by Iran and its proxies against Kuwait, and said the Abdali border crossing was affected. According to Jin10, the ministry made the statement.

data.title: US Wheat Farmers Face Heat as Prices Hit Three-Year High
data.description:
Scorching heat is threatening wheat yields in North Dakota as harvest nears, even as crop prices climb to a three-year high, according to Bloomberg.

The price rally comes as farmers survey fields that could be hit by lower output from the hot weather.

data.title: Pubs Cheer Business Rates Relief While Other Firms Left Out in the Cold
data.description:
Andy Burnham’s 20% business rates discount for pubs, social clubs and live music venues was welcomed by hospitality bosses, with tax experts saying it could save the average pub more than £1,000, according to BBC. The relief comes on top of emergency business rates support introduced in January.

But other High Street operators said they would miss out and urged ministers to widen the package. Keith Bott, who runs the Titanic Brewery and nine pubs across the Midlands, said pubs face pressure from higher National Insurance, wage costs, inflation and living costs, while Ruth Dawson of HD3 Fitness Centre in Huddersfield said her gym had been “left out in the cold”. Nick Smith, who runs the Ludoquist board game cafe and bar in Croydon, said he feared being excluded because his venue may not meet the government’s definition of a pub. Sam Lamiroy, who runs 45 Queen Street in Penzance, said a cut in VAT from 20% to 10% would be more effective than the business rates relief.

data.title: Jim Cramer Warns Wall Street Is Underestimating Oil and AI Spending Risks
data.description:
According to CNBC, Jim Cramer said Wall Street may be underestimating a growing set of risks for stocks, including higher oil prices and heavier spending on artificial intelligence. Speaking on "Squawk on the Street" before Thursday's market open, he said he was struggling to find reasons to buy and had plenty of reasons to sell.

He pointed to a weak session in which the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell more than 400 points, the S&P 500 dropped more than 1%, and the Nasdaq Composite lost about 2%. Cramer also said the S&P 500 was down just 1.5% from its June 2 record close as of Wednesday's finish, while the Nasdaq was about 5% below its June 2 peak and the Dow was about 1.6% under its July 6 record close.

Cramer said Brent crude surged more than 6% to above $100 a barrel after Houthi militants in Yemen said they attacked two Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea, and after President Donald Trump warned the U.S. would retaliate and bomb Iranian infrastructure. He said the move in crude lifted inflation concerns and pushed the 10-year Treasury yield to its highest level since January. The Federal Reserve's policy committee is due to meet next week, and CME FedWatch put the odds of a quarter-point rate increase at 38%, up from 12% a week earlier.

He also said Alphabet's latest quarter highlighted the cost of the AI buildout. Alphabet recently said it planned to raise roughly $85 billion through stock sales, on top of higher debt issuance this year.

data.title: AMD Expects Global Data Center CPU Market to Reach $220 Billion by 2030
data.description: According to Jin10, AMD said the global data center CPU market is expected to reach $220 billion by 2030.

data.title: Pantheon Electric Eyes Copper Acquisitions for Data Center Demand
data.description: Pantheon Electric plans to expand through acquisitions to meet rising demand from artificial intelligence data centers and grid upgrades, according to Bloomberg.
